title: referrals — trait composition
description: Six owned traits. `BelongsToReferralProgram` is the FK trait for downstream models (ReferralCode, Referral). `BelongsToReferral` is the FK trait for ReferralReward, ReferralFraudFlag, and future downstream (e.g. a marketing event tied to a specific referral). `HasReferralCode` / `HasReferrals` / `HasReferralRewards` / `HasReferralFraudFlags` are inverse-side conveniences on the parent models. Order matters when composed with `BelongsToTenant`: tenant MUST come first so subsequent traits observe the tenant context.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `referral_program_id` FK. Adds the `program()` belongsTo relation and a `booted()` hook that fills `referral_program_id = active_referral_program_id()` on `creating` when the field is null AND the current request carries a resolved active program. Refuses to save a row whose `referral_program_id` points at a program in a different tenant than the row itself.
composition_notes: Order: BelongsToTenant must appear BEFORE BelongsToReferralProgram.

owned.description: Mixed into models carrying a `referral_id` FK (ReferralReward, ReferralFraudFlag). Adds the `referral()` belongsTo relation + refuses cross-tenant writes.
usedBy: marketing::MarketingEvent (Wave 5 — the marketing event created on ReferralSignedUp/ReferralVested carries a referral_id for revenue attribution)

owned.description: Composed onto the User model. Adds `->referralCode()` (hasOne over ReferralCode for the active program with owner_user_id = self.id) + `->referralCodes()` (hasMany for cross-program). Convenience: `$user->referralCode->code` for the display code.

Composed onto the ReferralProgram model. Adds `->codes()`, `->referrals()`, `->activeReferrals()` (scope filter status NOT IN terminal set), `->completedReferrals()` (scope filter status IN ('rewarded'))

Composed onto the User model (for recipient rewards) AND onto the Referral model. On User: `->referralRewards()` (hasMany filtered on recipient_type='user' AND recipient_id=self.id). On Referral: `->rewards()` (hasMany). Dual-composition — same relation shape, different scoping predicate.

Composed onto the Referral model. Adds `->fraudFlags()` (hasMany) + `->activeFraudFlags()` (scope filter disposition IS NULL — unreviewed) + `->hasBlockingFraudFlag()` (bool — severity=block AND disposition != false_positive).

framework.reason: All five owned models. Retention pipeline handles hard-delete post-retention window.

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

activity.reason: Referral + ReferralReward lifecycle transitions feed the staff-visible activity feed. NOT on ReferralFraudFlag (high volume + already surfaces via ReferralFraudDetected event).

referral_program.notes: Order: BelongsToTenant first; HasSlug generates the tenant-scoped slug on create. HasReferrals is the inverse-side convenience.

referral_code.notes: NO HasActivityLog — code CRUD is admin-facing but does not surface in the staff activity feed.

referral.notes: The referrer + referred + recipient morphTo relations are declared inline on the model, not via a trait. Auditable applies because every state transition is compliance-relevant.

referral_reward.notes: Auditable is critical — every status transition is a financial-record mutation. Clawback path uses SoftDeletes to preserve the ledger.

referral_fraud_flag.notes: NO SoftDeletes — fraud flags are compliance-retained 7 years HARD. NO HasActivityLog — high volume + already surfaces via ReferralFraudDetected. Auditable is required for the 7-year fraud audit trail.
